bull_thesis: KBR is splitting in two, and the parts are worth meaningfully more than the bundled whole. On January 4, 2027 the company will spin off its Mission Technology Solutions arm (government services - national security and space, roughly $5.8 billion of revenue) as a separate public company, leaving 'New KBR' with the high-margin Sustainable Technology Solutions business (process-technology licensing for ammonia, chemicals, refining and clean energy, about $2.2 billion of revenue at a ~22% margin). Today the market values the combined company at a single blended multiple of about 10x EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization) - a conglomerate discount that flatters neither half. Once they trade separately, the technology licensor can be valued against richer process-tech and clean-energy peers (~11-14x) and the government-services half against defense-services comparables CACI, Leidos and Booz Allen (~10-12x). The catalyst is hard and dated: the board approved it unanimously in September 2025, the standalone leadership team (CEO-designate Michael LaRouche and CFO-designate Nicholas Veasey) was named on June 25, 2026, and management has reaffirmed the January 2027 completion. Naming the standalone executives is the concrete step companies take only when a spin is genuinely proceeding. With the stock at roughly $33 - near its 52-week low of $29.94 and well below both the Street's ~$46 average target and a full parts value in the high-$70s - the asymmetry is wide and the trigger is on the calendar.
bear_thesis: The case fails mainly through delay, not collapse. The timeline has already slipped once - original guidance was 'mid-to-late 2026' and the effective date has moved to January 4, 2027 - and a further slip would push the re-rating out and let the discount linger. The spun-off government-services company is the lower-margin half (about 10%), will carry standalone overhead and likely debt as a brand-new public entity, and may not re-rate cleanly to an 11x peer multiple right away; a newly public, lower-margin contractor often trades at a discount until it proves its standalone numbers. The full standalone financial carve-outs in the Form 10 registration statement are the swing factor for sentiment into the event, and weak disclosures could compress the SpinCo multiple. Energy-transition process-tech multiples for the New KBR half are also contested and could derate if clean-energy sentiment sours. In short, even though the businesses keep operating regardless (there is no deal to break), the spread can stay open longer than expected and the rich end of the parts math may not be realized on day one.
sop_bull: Favorable parts value if both halves re-rate toward peer medians. New KBR / Sustainable Technology Solutions: ~$479M trailing EBITDA at ~13x (high-margin process-tech licensor) = ~$6.2B enterprise value. SpinCo / Mission Technology Solutions: ~$571M trailing EBITDA at ~11-12x (defense-services peers) = ~$6.3-6.9B enterprise value. Combined enterprise value ~$12.5-13.1B; less ~$1.8B net debt over ~127M shares implies roughly $84-89 per share at a full re-rate. A more conservative, day-one realization aligns with the published $53 target and Street ~$46-47 - the realistic event-win value, with the high-$70s+ as upside optionality if the re-rate runs.
sop_bear: Verified downside floor is the standalone-bundle value, because there is no deal to break - both businesses keep operating. At ~$33 the whole company trades at ~10x EBITDA and ~10-11x earnings, already pricing the conglomerate discount, generating just over $1B of combined EBITDA and paying a ~2% dividend. The stock has held a $30-34 band through 2026 near its 52-week low of $29.94. Conservative parts (STS ~$479M EBITDA at 11x = ~$5.3B; MTS ~$571M at 9.5x = ~$5.4B; combined ~$10.7B EV less ~$1.8B net debt over 127M shares) still supports the high-$60s on a depressed basis, so the ~$33.2 floor is the trough trading price, not an intrinsic floor - you are buying a profitable, cash-generative defense-plus-tech conglomerate at a single-digit-to-low-double-digit multiple.
sop_breakdown: Base case ~$49 per share, probability-weighted between the verified target zone (~$53 dossier / ~$46-47 Street) at ~75% odds the spin completes on schedule and re-rates partially, and the ~$33 standalone-bundle floor at ~25% odds of a meaningful slip / muted re-rate. Parts: New KBR / Sustainable Technology Solutions ~$479M EBITDA x ~12x = ~$5.7B EV; SpinCo / Mission Technology Solutions ~$571M EBITDA x ~11x = ~$6.3B EV; combined ~$12B EV less ~$1.8B net debt / ~127M shares. Full re-rate optionality to the high-$70s if both halves reach peer medians.
risk_reward: Upside to the ~$53 target is about +59% from ~$33.37; downside to the ~$33.2 floor is roughly flat (about -0.5%). Asymmetry is strongly positive - roughly $20 of upside to target against essentially no downside to the verified trough floor (defined risk). To the base-case ~$49 fair value, ~+47% with the floor at the current price.
catalyst_summary: Tax-free spin-off of Mission Technology Solutions into a separate public company, effective January 4, 2027 - live price ~$33 sits well below the ~$53 parts target with a real floor at the standalone-bundle price (~$33), a wide, defined-risk asymmetry.
dated_milestone: Separation effective January 4, 2027; SpinCo CEO/CFO-designate named June 25, 2026 (LaRouche joins Sep 24, 2026; Veasey July 1, 2026); remaining gates are IRS tax ruling, SEC Form 10 effectiveness, and final board approval.
forcing_function: The January 4, 2027 separation date - the hard, scheduled event on which KBR splits into two independently traded companies, forcing each half to be valued against its own peer group and unlocking the conglomerate discount. Interim re-rate triggers: the Form 10 registration filing with standalone carve-out financials and the when-issued trading window ahead of the spin.
consensus_delta: Wall Street still values KBR as one blended company at roughly 10x EBITDA and ~10-11x earnings - the average analyst target of ~$46 implies the market is only part-way to pricing the separation, and the live price near $33 (a 52-week low) prices essentially none of it. The disagreement is structural, not a story: the bundle's single multiple flatters neither a high-margin (~22%) technology licensor nor a stable government-services contractor. Verified trailing segment EBITDA (STS ~$479M, MTS ~$571M) valued against the correct peer sets (process-tech/clean-energy at 11-14x for New KBR; CACI/Leidos/Booz Allen at 10-12x for SpinCo) sums to ~$12B of enterprise value, or the high-$70s per share at a full re-rate - well above where the Street sits. The market is underpricing both the certainty of the dated January 2027 trigger and the size of the discount it unlocks.
valley_of_death: Low. There is no cash-burn hump, no debt-maturity wall forcing the timeline, and no financing contingency - both businesses are profitable (combined ~$1B+ EBITDA) and the company pays a ~2% dividend throughout. The near-term risks are sentiment, not solvency: (1) a further slip in the January 2027 date (the timeline already moved one quarter from the original mid-2026 guidance), which would delay the re-rate rather than break the thesis; and (2) weak standalone carve-out financials in the Form 10 that could compress the SpinCo multiple before the spin. Neither decimates free cash flow; the floor (~$33, the current trough) holds because the operating businesses keep running regardless.
positioning_washout: Limited and largely behind us. KBR has spent most of 2026 in a $30-34 band near its 52-week low, so weak-handed and momentum holders have already been shaken out - the stock is down sharply from its $52 high. The spin itself creates mechanical flow rather than forced selling: when the separation completes, index funds and government-services-focused mandates that want SpinCo (and energy-transition mandates that want New KBR) will need to establish positions, and some holders of the combined name may rotate - that index/mandate reshuffle around January 2027 is a source of buying support, not a liquidation overhang. The dossier's index-flow tag points to this re-rating mechanic.
moderator_conclusion: This is the funnel working as intended - a defined-risk, dated special situation that earns an aggressive-entry call rather than a reflexive compounder cap. The catalyst is a hard, scheduled corporate event: KBR splits into two independent public companies on January 4, 2027, the board approved it unanimously, the standalone leadership team was named on June 25, 2026, and management has reaffirmed the timeline. The remaining gates (IRS tax ruling, SEC Form 10 effectiveness, board sign-off) are customary, not regulatory or financing hurdles - there is no deal that can be voted down, so the risk is slippage, not failure. The live price near $33 sits at a 52-week trough, well below both the Street's ~$46 target and a parts value (~$12B enterprise value, high-$70s per share at a full re-rate) that the conglomerate discount is suppressing. Critically, the floor is real and close: with no deal to break, the businesses keep generating $1B+ of EBITDA, so ~$33 is the standalone-bundle trough, giving roughly $20 of upside to the $53 target against essentially no downside to the floor. I dock conviction from the maximum only because the timeline has already slipped one quarter and the lower-margin SpinCo half may not re-rate cleanly on day one - delay risk, not break risk. Buy now: a live, dated catalyst inside the window, price well below the event-resolved value, with a verified floor. Value conviction matches at 4 - the margin of safety to the verified floor is wide because you are paying the trough price for two profitable franchises.
interrogator_dossier:
# KBR, Inc. (KBR) - Event-Driven Special Situation Dossier

**Setup:** Sum-of-the-parts spin-off. KBR is splitting itself in two. The thesis is the gap between today's blended price (~$33) and what the two halves are worth once they trade separately and each gets valued against its own peer group.

## 1. THE EVENT - what is being separated

On September 24, 2025, KBR's board unanimously approved a plan to spin off its Mission Technology Solutions business (the government-services arm - national security and space, roughly $5.8 billion in annual revenue and ~20,000 employees) into a separate, independent, publicly traded company. What remains - 'New KBR' - keeps the Sustainable Technology Solutions business, a high-margin licensing and process-technology franchise (ammonia, chemicals, refining, clean-energy / circular-economy process tech, with 85-plus licensed processes).

The logic is a classic conglomerate-discount unlock: a slow-growth-but-stable government services contractor and a high-margin, IP-driven energy-transition technology licensor do not belong in the same wrapper. Each appeals to a different buyer, and the market currently values the bundle at a single blended multiple (~10x EBITDA, ~10-11x earnings) that flatters neither.

## 2. CATALYST STATUS - is it real, dated, and binding?

This is the strongest part of the case. The catalyst is a firm, scheduled corporate event with a named completion date and a named management team:

- Completion date: January 4, 2027 - a hard, dated event roughly six months out.
- June 25, 2026: KBR named the leadership of the spun-off company - Michael LaRouche (ex-Serco/SAIC/Raytheon/Lockheed) as CEO-designate and Nicholas Veasey (ex-MAG Aerospace CFO) as CFO-designate. Naming the standalone team is a concrete step companies take only when a spin is genuinely proceeding.
- July 1, 2026 / September 24, 2026: Veasey and LaRouche join to stand up the independent company.
- Earnings-call confirmation: management reaffirmed the separation is on schedule.

Remaining gates are the customary ones for a tax-free U.S. spin: a favorable IRS private-letter ruling / tax opinion, the effectiveness of the Form 10 registration statement, and final board sign-off. None is an antitrust hurdle or financing contingency - there is no acquirer and no deal that can be voted down. The risk is slippage, not failure.

Timing nuance: original September 2025 guidance said 'mid-to-late 2026,' and the date has since moved to early January 2027 - a roughly one-quarter slip, mild for a spin of this size. A hard, dated, board-approved tax-free spin inside a defined window.

## 3. THE FLOOR - what backstops the price if the event disappoints

The downside is well-defined because there is no deal to break - the two businesses keep operating. The floor is the standalone-bundle value, not zero. KBR has traded a $30-34 band through 2026, near its 52-week low of $29.94 (high $52.23). At ~$33 the whole company trades at ~10x EBITDA and ~10-11x earnings - the market already prices the conglomerate discount. The combined business generates just over $1 billion of EBITDA and pays a ~2% dividend, so the price is supported by cash earnings. The verified floor is roughly the current trough, ~$33.

## 4. THE SPREAD - upside to the parts

Using verified trailing segment EBITDA:
- New KBR / Sustainable Technology Solutions: ~$479M EBITDA, ~22% margin on ~$2.2B revenue; process-tech/clean-energy peers ~11-14x; at ~12x ~$5.7B enterprise value.
- SpinCo / Mission Technology Solutions: ~$571M EBITDA on ~$5.8B revenue (~10% margin); CACI/Leidos/Booz Allen at ~10-12x; at ~11x ~$6.3B enterprise value.

Combined enterprise value ~$12B; less ~$1.7-1.9B net debt over ~127M shares implies the high-$70s to mid-$80s per share at a full re-rate - above the published $53 target and the Street's ~$46-47. Targets sit below the full re-rate because spins rarely close the gap day one, SpinCo carries standalone costs/debt, and the STS energy-transition multiple is contested. Verified target zone ~$47-53, with optionality to the $70s.

## 5. RED / GREEN FLAGS

- Green: dated completion (Jan 4, 2027), standalone team named, board-approved, tax-free, no antitrust/financing gate, profitable on both sides, price at the trough.
- Green: demonstrable conglomerate discount - blended ~10x vs. parts 11-14x.
- Amber: timeline slipped one quarter; a further slip delays rather than kills the thesis.
- Amber: SpinCo is the lower-margin half (~10%) and will carry standalone costs/debt; its 11x multiple assumes a clean re-rate.
- Amber: Form 10 carve-out financials are the swing factor for sentiment into the event.

## 6. MOAT / SECULAR (largely N/A)

New KBR's technology-licensing IP is a narrow but real moat (switching costs, installed-base licensing); the government-services half is a stable scale/clearance moat. No terminal secular threat - both halves sit in supported end-markets (energy-transition process tech, national-security/space). The cheapness is a structure-and-bundling artifact, not a melting base.

**Bottom line:** A hard, dated, board-approved tax-free spin (effective January 4, 2027) with standalone leadership named, a live price (~$33) at its 52-week trough and well below Street targets (~$46) and a parts value in the high-$70s, with a real floor at the current standalone-bundle price. Strong defined-risk asymmetry; main risk is further timeline slip, which delays rather than kills the thesis.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none
